sample.title: How Data Centers in Space Could Change AI
sample.description:
“Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier.

How to Build a Data Center in Space: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn
Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos plan to make AI extraterrestrial.

Building data centers in space is still a relatively nascent idea, but it’s gaining traction and financial backing, as energy and space constraints limit the expansion of massive computing facilities here on Earth. Earlier this year, in the latest leg of their decades-long space race, Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin both announced plans to build and launch so-called orbital data centers.

Learn more: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn

00:00-01:36 The AI data center squeeze
01:37-03:43 Starcloud’s big ambition
03:44-04:17 Why data centers in space make sense
04:18-8:15 The engineering challenges and possible solutions
08:16-09:28 Reducing the cost of launch
09:29-09:57 Global race to get there 
09:58-11:47 China’s different approach
11:48-13:21 Who will control the next internet?

sample.title: Dario Amodei Addresses Reports of Claude’s Role in US Military Strikes
sample.description:
On The Circuit with Emily Chang, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei addresses reports that the US military is using Claude as part of the US-Israel war with Iran, including during a strike in Iran that destroyed a girls' school, killing more than 150 people. 

Amodei concedes "terrible" mistakes will be made but defends the integration of AI in defense by emphasizing that "a human makes the final call."

sample.title: Inside Indonesia's Market Meltdown
sample.description:
Indonesia was once seen as a darling of investors. At $1.5 trillion, Indonesia’s GDP is more than that of Singapore and Thailand combined. Until recently, Indonesia’s stock exchange was the biggest in the region. 

But fears of corruption and policy missteps in markets and government under President Prabowo Subianto are raising concern about the country’s economic future.

sample.description:

sample.title: Where Markets Think Fed Chair Warsh Is Taking Interest Rates
sample.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s management of assets, inflation and public communications. Markets are pricing in higher odds for future rate hikes, rather than rate cuts as emphasized by Chair Warsh in confirmation hearings. CNBC’s Matt Peterson, Steve Liesman and Jeff Cox report.

sample.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
sample.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

Chapters:
0:00-54:00 Introduction
00:55-03:16 Chapter 1: Computing Costs
03:17-05:27 Chapter 2: Demand
05:28-07:21 Chapter 3: Setting a Benchmark

sample.title: Why KFC Has Fallen Behind In The U.S.
sample.description:
Chains like Chick-fil-A and Raising Cane's have become fast food mainstays as Americans continue to crave chicken. Yet KFC has become the fourth-largest U.S. chicken chain by market share despite the company saying that ‘it made chicken famous.’ The U.S. has also slipped to its third-largest market behind China and Europe. Now it's rebranding its logos, menu and restaurants as it hopes to reinvigorate sales and cut through the crowded fast-food sector.  CNBC’s Amelia Lucas has more on the company’s innovations.

Produced Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Amelia Lucas
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images, KFC

sample.title: What To Expect From Trump’s Trip To The G7 Summit In France
sample.description:
President Trump is set to travel to France for the upcoming G7 summit, which will take place from June 15-17. Markets will be watching for signs of stability as the summit takes place against the backdrop of the Iran war, the Russia-Ukraine war and tensions between the U.S. and Europe over tariffs. CNBC’s Megan Cassella breaks down what to expect from the summit.

Reporter: Megan Cassella
Produced by: Macklin Fishman, Ashlee Trujillo
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: The Next Phase Of The Obesity Drug Race
sample.description:
Five years after Wegovy’s FDA approval, the obesity drug market has evolved rapidly. Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly introduced GLP-1 pills this year and others like Structure Therapeutics and Zealand Pharma are trying to fight for market share.  But the space is getting crowded. CNBC’s Angelica Peebles has the story from the American Diabetic Association’s conference in New Orleans.

Chapters:
00:00 - 00:37 Introduction
00:38 - 03:10 Mounting competition 
03:11 - 5:38 Next steps in innovation

sample.title: Kevin Warsh's Fed is not expected to make any change to rates for a while: CNBC Fed Survey
sample.description:
Amid heightened anticipation, Kevin Warsh will head his first meeting as Federal Reserve chairman but is expected do very little, at least initially, according to the latest CNBC Fed Survey.

CNBC's Steve Liesman has the details.

sample.title: Veteran traders stunned as SpaceX fans bet on 80% overnight gains
sample.description:
Believers in Elon Musk's astronomic vision for SpaceX are storming options on the stock as punters place cheap bets on massive moves and total premium surpasses trading in monster index ETFs.

CNBC's Oliver Renick has the details.

sample.title: Snap unveils $2,195 AR glasses as CEO Evan Spiegel bets on post-smartphone future
sample.description:
Snap is launching Specs, the company’s first AR glasses geared toward the broader public instead of developers.

"They're both highly wearable, but also incredibly capable for immersive computing," CEO Evan Spiegel says.

sample.title: Ukrainian drone strike starts fire at Moscow region's largest refinery
sample.description:
A Ukrainian drone attack started a fire at the refinery that is the largest fuel supplier to the Moscow region. Two industry sources told Reuters that it had halted operations.

The sources said the strike on Gazprom Neft's refinery in southeast Moscow damaged a primary refining facility that accounts for 53% of ‌the plant's capacity. Footage showed a fierce blaze and dense black smoke rising from the refinery after the attack.

Social media video also showed a Ukrainian drone hitting a building under construction in the nearby Russian town of Kotelniki.
 
#ukraine #russia #war #moscow #dronestrike #energysecurity

https://reut.rs/4eeegBN

sample.title: Heatwaves, early springs threatening migratory birds in Toronto
sample.description:
People flock to Toronto's Tommy Thompson Park in hopes of spotting migratory birds, but the park reported a significant drop in spring and summer bird banding totals compared with historical averages, linking the decline directly to climate volatility in its 2025 annual report.

sample.title: The UK government is a considering an overnight social media curfew. #BBCNews
Trump says Iran will 'never have a nuclear weapon’, as he attends G7 summit | BBC News
sample.description:
US President Donald Trump has said Iran will "never have a nuclear weapon" in his preliminary deal to end the war. 
 
Speaking at the G7 summit, Trump also said that without him "there would be no Israel" and criticised Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu. 
 
Fighting between Hezbollah and Israel has continued, and Iran has said Israeli troops must withdraw from Lebanon. 
 
Netanyahu said his troops will not be withdrawing and the framework deal was reached between Iran and the US, not Israel. 

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#US #Iran #Israel #BBCNews

sample.title: How will YouTube be affected by the UK government’s social media ban for under-16s? #BBCNews
On the front line against Ebola in DR Congo | Global News Podcast
sample.description:
Close to 800 people have been confirmed to have contracted Ebola in the east of the Democratic Republic of Congo. Of those, about a quarter have died. 
 
Health care workers are struggling to curb the spread of the deadly disease, but they have also witnessed patients recovering - giving hope to their fight against Ebola.
 
We spoke to the BBC's Anne Soy who's been to the epicentre of the outbreak in the DRC, in Ituri province.
 
New episodes of the Global News Podcast are published twice a day. You can listen here: https://link.mgln.ai/GNP-YT
 
And you can find more of our YouTube episodes here: BBC World Service - Global News Podcast - Downloads

#Ebola #DRCongo #BBCNews
